title: libertia edgariae
intro: Libertia edgariae, also known as the golden grass or New Zealand iris, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. The plant's rhizomes and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Libertia edgariae are said to include relieving arthritis symptoms, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which may be used to support the body's natural healing processes.
